sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते॥
transliteration:
sarva-bhūta-sthitaṃ yo māṃ bhajaty-ekatvam-āsthitaḥ |
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate ||
literal_meaning: Who, established in the vision of oneness, worships Me as the indwelling Self in all beings—though engaged in any activity—such a yogī abides in Me.
contextual_meaning: When identity expands from the isolated ego to the shared Self in everyone, every action becomes an expression of that unity; the person is psychologically ‘home’ regardless of outer role or circumstance.
psychological_state: Integrated self-transcendence: the ego remains functional yet is experienced as a transparent vessel for a single field of awareness shared with all others.
trigger_scenario: A nurse working 12-hour COVID shifts feels an unexpected calm unity with every patient—seeing the same awareness behind each pair of eyes—allowing her to stay centered while performing triage.
anchor_text: See yourself in others and serve the Self in every action.
